title: Implement TUI data model and panel formatting
description: Create the TUI support module with deterministic helpers for filtering beads, building parent/child tree rows, preserving selection when possible, summarizing status counts, and formatting the detail panel from bead and handoff data. Keep rendering-oriented data preparation separate from the runtime loop so snapshot-style tests stay simple.
acceptance_criteria: Add a dedicated TUI module that exposes small helper functions such as row collection, tree building, detail formatting, and status/footer formatting.
Default filtering includes only ready, in_progress, blocked, and deferred/actionable states defined for the console, with support for all and per-state filter modes including done.
Tree ordering is deterministic: roots first, descendants indented, siblings sorted by bead id ascending.

Detail formatting includes bead id, title, status, type, agent, parent id, feature root id, dependencies, acceptance criteria, block reason, file summaries, and latest handoff summary fields needed by the spec.
Helpers read bead data through existing storage/model structures and do not introduce network calls or duplicate repository scanning outside storage access.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` with console filter modes, storage-backed row collection, deterministic parent/child tree traversal, selection resolution helpers, status/footer formatting, and detail panel formatting from bead plus handoff data. Added targeted tests in `tests/test_orchestrator.py` covering filter behavior, tree ordering, selection preservation, and detail/footer output.
handoff_summary.remaining: Runtime loop integration and review signoff are still separate follow-up work.
handoff_summary.risks: The linked TUI spec file was not present in the workspace, so the grouped filter mapping follows the current model surface: `actionable` maps to `open` + `ready`, and `deferred` maps to `handed_off`. If the runtime console expects different group semantics or labels, the integration bead may need a small adjustment.
handoff_summary.next_action: Wire the runtime TUI loop to these helpers and confirm the filter group semantics against the intended console UX.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream TUI/runtime work should reuse the helper filter constants and footer/detail formatting paths instead of re-implementing them, otherwise selection and status text can drift.

conflict_risks: Downstream TUI/runtime work should reuse the helper filter constants and footer/detail formatting paths instead of re-implementing them, otherwise selection and status text can drift.

execution_history.summary: Implemented a new storage-backed TUI helper module for filtered bead rows, deterministic tree ordering, selection preservation, footer/status formatting, and detail panel rendering, with focused unit coverage.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
